problem_title: Beneficiary data gaps slow compliance reviews
problem_description: Incomplete or inconsistent beneficiary information within Travel Rule workflows creates delays and manual follow-ups for compliance teams. This fragmentation complicates case management, weakens audit trails, and increases the risk of missing critical data during regulatory review.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY links originator and beneficiary details, Travel Rule message status, exception flags and audit evidence into one operational workflow for streamlined compliance reviews and case management.
workflow_title: From Travel Rule exceptions to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY connects Travel Rule message status and beneficiary data completeness with exception handling, routing reviews, case creation and evidence preservation for compliance teams.

workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for Travel Rule compliance?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects Travel Rule messages, originator and beneficiary data, exceptions, cases and audit evidence into one structured compliance workflow for regulated digital asset businesses.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle beneficiary data completeness?
faq_2_answer: SFTY validates beneficiary data completeness by reviewing message fields, routing incomplete data into cases for manual review and escalation within the Travel Rule workflow.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve Travel Rule evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves evidence by maintaining detailed logs of message routing, decision records, case histories and exception handling related to Travel Rule operations.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with Travel Rule providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates with multiple Travel Rule providers, aggregating their outputs to support connected routing, status tracking, exception handling and compliance workflows.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace Travel Rule service providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY works above Travel Rule providers, connecting and structuring their outputs without replacing the underlying service providers.
final_title: Review beneficiary data workflows with SFTY
